Cataract Treatment in Kochi

Cataract is a common eye condition where the natural lens becomes cloudy, leading to blurry or reduced vision. It usually occurs due to aging but may also result from eye injuries, medical conditions like diabetes, or long-term use of steroids. Cataracts can affect one or both eyes and develop gradually, causing difficulty in reading, driving, or seeing clearly at night.

Common Symptoms of Cataract:

Blurred or cloudy vision

Sensitivity to bright lights or glare

Faded or yellowed colors

Difficulty seeing at night

Double vision in one eye

Frequent changes in eyeglass

Advanced Cataract Treatments at Giridhar Eye Institute

At Giridhar Eye Institute in Kochi, we offer state-of-the-art cataract surgery techniques for faster recovery, enhanced precision, and excellent visual outcomes.

Phacoemulsification (Phaco Surgery)

Phaco is the most widely performed cataract surgery. An ultrasonic device is used to break the cloudy lens into tiny fragments, which are then gently suctioned out. A clear intraocular lens (IOL) is placed in its position.

Advantages:

Quick recovery

Small incision

Minimal discomfort

Low risk of infection

Microincision Cataract Surgery (MICS)

MICS is an advanced version of phacoemulsification using incisions smaller than 2 mm. This minimally invasive technique improves post-surgery comfort and visual results.

Advantages:

Smaller incision

Reduced induced astigmatism

Faster healing

Femtosecond Laser-Assisted Cataract Surgery (Femto)

This bladeless, laser-guided technique enhances the precision of critical steps like corneal incision, capsulotomy, and lens fragmentation. Femto cataract surgery ensures greater accuracy, especially in complex cases.

Adantages:

Greater precision

Reduced trauma to eye tissues

Better refractive outcomes

Types of Intraocular Lenses (IOLs)

After cataract removal, an artificial lens (IOL) is implanted to restore clear vision. Giridhar Eye Institute offers a wide range of premium and customized IOL options based on your visual needs and lifestyle.

Monofocal IOLs

Provide sharp vision at one set distance, typically far. Reading glasses may still be required.

Ideal for: Patients who don’t mind using glasses for near tasks.

Multifocal IOLs

Designed with multiple focusing zones to help you see at near, intermediate, and far distances.

Benefits: Reduces dependence on spectacles, though some patients may notice glare or halos at night.

Toric IOLs

Specifically designed to correct astigmatism, improving visual clarity across all distances.

Best for: Patients with corneal astigmatism.

Extended Depth of Focus (EDOF) IOLs

Provide a seamless range of vision, improving intermediate and near tasks while minimizing glare compared to multifocal lenses.

Advantages: Better contrast, fewer visual disturbances, improved range of focus.

Glaucoma Treatment in Kochi – Advanced Eye Care at Giridhar Eye Institute

Glaucoma is a group of eye conditions that cause damage to the optic nerve, often due to elevated intraocular pressure (IOP). It is one of the leading causes of irreversible blindness worldwide. Often called the “silent thief of sight,” glaucoma may not show symptoms until significant vision loss has occurred—typically starting with peripheral vision.

At Giridhar Eye Institute, Kochi, we specialize in early diagnosis and advanced treatment of glaucoma using state-of-the-art technology and experienced eye specialists.

Types of Glaucoma

Primary Open-Angle Glaucoma (POAG)

The most common type where the eye’s drainage system becomes inefficient, leading to gradual pressure buildup.

Angle-Closure Glaucoma

A less common but more serious condition where the eye’s drainage angle suddenly becomes blocked, causing a rapid rise in IOP—often requiring emergency care.

Secondary Glaucoma

Caused by other conditions such as trauma, inflammation, eye surgery, or steroid use.

Glaucoma Diagnosis at Giridhar Eye Institute

Accurate and early diagnosis is essential to protect your vision. We use a combination of advanced investigations to assess eye pressure, optic nerve health, and visual field function.

Tonometry (IOP Measurement)

Goldmann Applanation Tonometry: Gold standard for precise pressure measurement.

Non-Contact Tonometry (Air Puff Test): Quick, no-contact IOP test.
Tono-Pen: Portable device ideal for children or irregular corneas.

Optical Coherence Tomography (OCT)

Captures high-resolution images of the optic nerve head and retinal nerve fiber layer (RNFL) to detect early nerve damage—even before vision is affected.

Fundus Photography & Optic Nerve Evaluation

Visual documentation of the optic disc helps in monitoring glaucomatous changes like cupping and nerve fiber thinning over time.

Visual Field Test (Perimetry)

Assesses peripheral vision and identifies areas of vision loss or scotomas, essential in glaucoma diagnosis and follow-up.

Gonioscopy

Examines the anterior chamber angle to differentiate between open-angle and angle-closure glaucoma, using a special lens.

Pachymetry

Measures corneal thickness, which influences the accuracy of IOP readings. It helps assess the risk of glaucoma progression.

Glaucoma Treatment Options

Medications

Prescription eye drops are usually the first line of defense to reduce eye pressure. They either decrease fluid production or improve drainage.

Laser Therapy

Laser Trabeculoplasty: Improves fluid outflow in open-angle glaucoma.

Laser Iridotomy: Creates a small opening in the iris to relieve pressure in angle-closure glaucoma.

Laser Cyclophotocoagulation: Reduces fluid production by targeting the ciliary body.

Surgical Treatment

For advanced cases or when medications/lasers are ineffective.

Trabeculectomy: Creates a new drainage channel to lower IOP.

Glaucoma Drainage Devices (Shunts or Valves): Implants that help continuously drain eye fluid and reduce pressure.

Glaucoma Services

Glaucoma is the leading cause of irreversible blindness in the world. The fact that it is hardly known to the majority of population is truly a sad state of affairs. In India around 12 million are affected of whom 1.1 million are blind. The illness is usually slowly progressing and patients are unaware of anything wrong with their eyes till significant visual loss has occurred. The initiative in glaucoma should be early diagnosis and prompt treatment with proper education of the patient regarding the grave nature of the disease. At GEI, we have specialists in this sub-specialty with all the necessary instruments for diagnosing this disease.

Early Diagnosis and Treatment

Early diagnosis and treatment of glaucoma prevent irreversible vision loss.

Specialized Glaucoma Care

Our specialists use advanced tools for effective glaucoma diagnosis and management.

Comprehensive Patient Education

We educate patients on glaucoma management and treatment options.

Advanced Diagnostic Equipment

GEI uses advanced instruments for precise glaucoma diagnosis and tailored treatment.

Frequently Asked Questions

Find answers to common questions about cataract and glaucoma, covering symptoms, treatments, and the importance of timely care.

Can cataracts or glaucoma be treated without surgery?

Cataracts can only be treated surgically. Glaucoma, on the other hand, can often be managed with medications or laser treatments to reduce eye pressure.

Are cataracts or glaucoma hereditary?

Yes, both cataracts and glaucoma can have a genetic component, meaning a family history may increase your risk.

Is vision loss from glaucoma reversible?

Unfortunately, any vision loss due to glaucoma is irreversible. Early detection and treatment are key to preventing further damage.

Can I have cataract surgery if I have glaucoma?

Yes, patients with glaucoma can still undergo cataract surgery, and in some cases, combined surgeries for both conditions may be performed.

How long is recovery after cataract surgery?

Recovery from cataract surgery is typically quick, with most patients resuming normal activities within a few days, though complete healing may take several weeks.

Does glaucoma always lead to blindness?

Not necessarily. With early detection and proper management, most people with glaucoma can preserve their vision throughout life.

How often should I be screened for glaucoma?

If you are at risk, annual eye exams are recommended. Those over 40 or with a family history should have regular screenings.

Can lifestyle changes help manage glaucoma?

Yes, a healthy lifestyle that includes regular exercise, a balanced diet, and avoiding smoking may help reduce the risk of glaucoma progression.
